patterns.description: An abstract domain is made perceptually legible through a surface system.
patterns.perceptual_meaning: The brand does not merely state a category; it gives the category a readable surface logic.
observable_signals: source names an abstract domain
record identifies a perceptual organizing frame
strategy or identity is linked to surface behavior
anti_patterns: treating the category itself as the perceptual pattern
repeating the source metaphor without observing surface behavior
turning concept translation into a quality claim

patterns.description: A perceptual reading is constrained by its evidence status.
patterns.perceptual_meaning: The pattern protects Brand3 from converting source language into confirmed visual truth.
observable_signals: facts, observations, interpretations and weak inferences are separated
medium-confidence observations are often copy-based
low-confidence inferences require human review
contradictions name the boundary between claim and signal
anti_patterns: calling a claim visible before a capture exists
treating public case language as product behavior
allowing low-confidence strategic intent into reusable language without review

patterns.description: The case frames movement through a guided path: gallery pacing, UI guidance, or design system guidance.
patterns.perceptual_meaning: Movement can be how a surface directs attention, sequence, identity, or system use.
observable_signals: path, guiding, threshold, transition, seamlessness, or navigation language
sequential gallery or lookbook-like movement
UI claims about guiding users
system claims about guiding multiple brand instances
anti_patterns: treating any animation as guided movement
treating the word guide as proof of interface clarity
ignoring friction if the path is claimed but not visible

patterns.description: Motion or implied motion is used to carry a concept rather than to decorate the surface.
patterns.perceptual_meaning: Movement becomes part of identity logic when it embodies the case's conceptual source.
observable_signals: motion production language tied to a concept
transitions described as meaningful
surface rhythm or repeated pacing supports a conceptual atmosphere
human review required when motion is only stated in copy
anti_patterns: treating production tools as perceptual evidence by themselves
calling motion concept-bearing without showing what concept it carries
overusing cinematic language for static surfaces

patterns.description: A system creates recognizable unity while preserving meaningful variation.
patterns.perceptual_meaning: Strong systems can make difference legible inside a controlled frame.
observable_signals: multi-brand components, foundations, shared structures, or repeated motifs
language about cohesion and unique personalities
repetition with controlled variation
need for variant, component, or sub-brand evidence
anti_patterns: treating uniformity as coherence
treating uniqueness claims as proven without variant evidence
calling repetition modular without component proof

patterns.description: A productive or unresolved gap appears between what the source claims and what the available evidence can support.
patterns.perceptual_meaning: The gap is a review object that prevents premature generalization.
observable_signals: contradictions marked as soft or unverified
claims that need more evidence
low-confidence inferences requiring human review
friction log entries about copy-only or system evidence
anti_patterns: treating every gap as a negative contradiction
letting uncertainty disappear in polished prose
resolving gaps by adding unsupported strategy language

patterns.description: The surface creates a felt transition from one state into another.
patterns.perceptual_meaning: Pacing behaves like a threshold when it changes how the viewer enters, exits, or interprets a system.
observable_signals: gallery-first sequencing
delayed or threshold-like CTA
transition language
motion or rhythm that marks passage between states
anti_patterns: treating any scroll sequence as threshold behavior
calling slow pacing premium without evidence
confusing atmospheric delay with missing product clarity

unstable_patterns.reason: Needs actual motion capture, not only production-method copy.
Currently mixes interface guidance, case-page pacing, and system guidance.
Strong for Grandvalira, but needs more system cases and component evidence.
Well supported in Charms, weaker in D4DATA.
Strong in Charms, still speculative outside case-page flows.

too_case_specific_patterns: Agentic games as soft social ritual

vocabulary_collisions.collision: Can mean visual identity system, product system, design system, ecosystem, or governance layer.
Can mean animation, transition, production method, cadence, or implied movement.
Can mean UI guidance, case-page pacing, strategic direction, or system governance.
Can mean component architecture, narrative flexibility, or brand extensibility.
Can mean visual consistency, multi-brand governance, or narrative unity.
Can mean repeated interaction, emotional cadence, or poetic copy.
Can mean restraint, polish, aspiration, or high production value.

registry_boundary: This registry is not a scoring rubric, prompt file, report language file, Visual Signature implementation, or runtime dependency.
